response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack/web engineer with 15+ years of experience, meeting the years-of-experience bar via the Bachelor's path. However, the role requires deep hardware-domain expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. His performance experience (low-latency DLang services, async I/O) is at the software/services layer, not silicon or deep-learning workload optimization, making this a significant domain mismatch for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad engineering experience across web, embedded, and high-throughput systems
Low-latency, high-performance systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers, caching)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and teaching/mentoring
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep-learning training/inference optimization background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not hardware performance architecture at principal level

key_factors.factor: General performance engineering and systems depth
